scene: A 1930s movie theater owner standing before a row of his small theaters, making a big fateful decision, gesturing with one arm. Period clothes, hat. Selective pop color: a small bit of golden popcorn somewhere in frame. No text, no letters, no numbers.

A long row of humble little 1930s movie theaters, each with a popcorn machine or popcorn cart out front, bustling and busy with little stick-figure people. Selective pop color: golden popcorn. No text, no letters, no numbers.

A few tall fancy ornate 1930s theaters with chandeliers and a snooty doorman turning his nose up at a popcorn cart, velvet ropes out front. No popcorn at these fancy ones. No text, no letters, no numbers.

The humble popcorn theaters two years later: packed happy crowds of stick-figure people, overflowing popcorn, coins and bills floating in the air. Thriving and joyful. Selective pop color: golden popcorn. No text, no letters, no numbers.
The fancy holdout theaters now boarded up, dark, shuttered with planks across the doors, cobwebbed and empty. The fall. No text, no letters, no numbers.

A giant glowing tub of popcorn held up like a round rescue lifesaver ring over a small struggling fancy theater that is reaching one arm up toward it too late, sinking. Selective pop color: golden popcorn. No text, no letters, no numbers.

Present day: an enormous tub of popcorn sitting on a king's throne wearing a small crown, towering over a tiny subservient little movie theater building beside it. Selective pop color: golden popcorn and a red crown. No text, no letters, no numbers.
